Frequently asked questions

settings.heading: What age are your kids' products suitable for?

settings.text: Our kids products are suitable for ages 2 years +

settings.heading: How do I charge my kids electric toothbrush?

settings.text: To charge your brush, open the protective silicone cover at the base of the brush. Insert your USB cable into the USB port and connect the cable to your plug. Charge your toothbrush for 4 hours prior to first use and for up to 2-3 hours post your first use. When the brush is charging, the charging light will appear red, and when fully charged, the charging light will change to green indicating that your brush is now fully charged and ready for use! Ensure the silicone cover is placed firmly back in place before use.

settings.heading: How often should I replace the head on my kids toothbrush?

settings.text: We recommend changing the kids toothbrush heads every 3 months.

settings.heading: Are the kids’ electric toothbrush heads recyclable?

settings.text: Yes, our toothbrush heads can be recycled.

settings.heading: How often should I clean my kids’ electric toothbrush?

settings.text: We recommend to rinse the brush head and bristles after each use, and to wipe the entire surface of the toothbrush handle with a damp cloth when needed.

settings.heading: Can my kids use the electric toothbrush with a loose tooth?

settings.text: Yes, but ensure they are brushing gently when brushing around the loose tooth!

settings.heading: Do your kids’ toothpastes contain fluoride?

settings.text: Yes, our toothpaste contains the active ingredient Sodium Fluoride used in the prevention of decay.

settings.heading: Do your kids' toothpastes contain any additives?

settings.text: No, our toothpastes are free from additives, any artificial dyes, free from Triclosan and SLS. Our pastes all contain natural flavours, are vegan friendly, cruelty free and our packaging is 100% recyclable.

settings.heading: What toothpaste should I choose for my kid?

settings.text: Both of our kids toothpastes have the same dentist approved ingredients, all your little one needs to do is pick their favourite flavour!

settings.heading: Any tips on brushing my kids' teeth?

settings.text:
Some of our top tips from Dr Lisa and Dr Vanessa include

Up to the age of 7 you should help your child in brushing their teeth to ensure that they’re brushing each tooth individually.
Ensure they are brushing for two minutes, twice a day every day
Make it fun! Play their favourite song when brushing to distract them from the task
Ensure they are using a fluoride based toothpaste to prevent decay
